working_on: Bake SWE-hardening rollout (Issues #65–#86) into engine/STATE.md as a phased adoption plan with dependency graph and trigger surface so future sessions can pick the next eligible item with full context. declared_scope: Single-deliverable session: append SWE-hardening rollout section to engine/STATE.md and add SWE-hardening rollout candidate to S-0124+ candidates list. The rollout indexes 22 GitHub Issues (#65–#86) filed at session-pre-S-0124 from the audit plan at ~/.claude/plans/not-a-working-session-sequential-twilight.md, organized into Tier 1 foundations (#65 #66 #68 #69 #67), Tier 2 judgment+observability (#70 #71 #72 #73 #74 #75-priority-urgent), Tier 3 composition+release+trigger-gated (#76 #77 #78 #80 #79 #82 #83 #84), and analysis-outcome additions (#81 #86 #85). Section adds explicit dependency table per tier, trigger-gated re-surface table tying Phase 6/9/collaborator-count milestones to specific Issues, and explicit non-adoptions table recording 9 confirmed-skip decisions from the ECC and addyosmani audits. Adoption discipline (ADR + MemPalace decision drawer + first-exercise readiness note + verification + posture-interaction reasoning) is baked into each Issue body so the future session opening any of them inherits everything needed to execute cold. No code changes, no migrations, no ADRs authored in this session — each Tier 1+ Issue is its own ADR-tracked downstream session.

scope_delivery.explanation: Plan delivered exactly as scoped, with mid-session feedback absorbed (user pushed back on hand-waving in initial plan; I revised to bake adoption discipline + verification + dependency surfacing into each of the 22 Issue bodies and into STATE.md, and to actually analyze the 'skip → cross-check' items rather than defer them). Concrete deliverables: (1) 22 GitHub Issues filed #65–#86 with full bodies (Context, Symptom, Proposed approach, Adoption discipline, Verification, Affected files, Cross-references) per engine/operations/issue-discipline.md; (2) STATE.md 'SWE-hardening rollout' section appended with per-tier dependency tables + trigger-gated re-surface table + explicit non-adoptions table (commit bde7cd4); (3) STATE.md 'Last build session' row updated to S-0124 + 'Next session work item' intro updated to S-0125 — open candidates; (4) ENGINE_LOG.md [Unreleased] section extended with S-0124 Added/Changed entries; (5) MemPalace diary entry filed in wing_claude topic paideia-swe-hardening-rollout (AAAK-compressed). Cross-check analyses: agent-skills documentation-and-adrs surfaced 4 real gaps → #81 (template enhancement) + #85 (API docs); git-workflow-and-versioning wholesale-skip confirmed but real revert/rollback gap extracted → #86; ci-cd-and-automation judgment patterns (no-skip philosophy, 10-min budget, flaky-test discipline, GitHub-Secrets-for-test-creds) baked directly into #68 body. Explicit non-adoptions: 9 items recorded as settled-skip decisions in STATE.md non-adoptions table.

outcome_summary: Authored the SWE-hardening rollout for Paideia comparing current posture against affaan-m/everything-claude-code (GitHub-side infrastructure templates) and addyosmani/agent-skills (engineering judgment frameworks). Filed 22 GitHub Issues #65–#86 across three tiers + analysis-outcome additions: Tier 1 foundations (#65 lockfile, #66 secret scanning, #68 CI mirror, #69 PR template + branch protection, #67 Dependabot); Tier 2 judgment + observability (#70 bandit, #71 pytest-cov, #72 issue templates, #73 /review skill, #74 /security-review skill, #75 frontend-ui-engineering [priority:urgent precondition for Phase 6]); Tier 3 composition + release + trigger-gated (#76 /ship multi-model, #77 doubt-driven evaluation, #78 .editorconfig, #80 CODEOWNERS, #79 perf, #82 release tagging, #83 SBOM, #84 monthly metrics); analysis-outcome additions (#81 ADR template enhancement, #86 revert/rollback discipline, #85 API docs). Each Issue body bakes in ADR requirement + MemPalace decision drawer + first-exercise readiness note where applicable per ADR 0053 + verification steps + posture-interaction reasoning so future sessions inherit everything cold. Baked the rollout structure into engine/STATE.md as a 'SWE-hardening rollout' section with per-tier dependency tables + trigger-gated re-surface table tying Phase 6/9 / deployable-artifact / collaborator-count milestones to specific Issues + explicit non-adoptions table recording 9 confirmed-skip decisions with reasoning. User feedback absorbed mid-session (no-hand-waving + analyze-the-skips + bake-into-STATE.md + surface-dependencies-when-met) drove the revision from initial plan to executable artifact. No code/migrations/ADRs in this session — each Tier 1+ Issue is its own ADR-tracked downstream session per the discipline embedded.
